mission.title: Install and Configure Kubefleet on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeFleet is an open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster application management solution that simplifies governance and workload management across multiple clusters. Installing KubeFleet allows you to efficiently manage applications in a hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

steps.title: Set up a Kind cluster for testing
steps.description:
KubeFleet uses a hub-spoke model. Create clusters for testing:
```bash
# Create hub cluster
kind create cluster --name hub

# Create member clusters
kind create cluster --name member1
kind create cluster --name member2
```

steps.title: Install KubeFleet on the hub cluster
steps.description:
Install the KubeFleet CRDs and controllers on the hub:
```bash
kubectl config use-context kind-hub
helm repo add kubefleet https://kubefleet-dev.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kubefleet kubefleet/kubefleet --namespace kubefleet-system --create-namespace
```
See https://kubefleet-dev.github.io/website/docs/getting-started/kind/ for the full quickstart.

steps.title: Join member clusters
steps.description:
Register member clusters with the hub:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: cluster.kubefleet.io/v1
kind: MemberCluster
metadata:
  name: member1
spec:
  identity:
    name: member1
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet-system
kubectl get memberclusters
```

resolution.summary: KubeFleet is installed on the hub cluster with member clusters joined for multi-cluster workload management.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeFleet
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall kubefleet -n kubefleet-system
kubectl delete namespace kubefleet-system
```

uninstall.title: Delete Kind clusters
uninstall.description:
```bash
kind delete cluster --name hub
kind delete cluster --name member1
kind delete cluster --name member2
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubefleet kubefleet/kubefleet --namespace kubefleet-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Member cluster not joining
troubleshooting.description:
Check the join agent logs on the member cluster:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=kubefleet-agent -n kubefleet-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Placement policy not scheduling
troubleshooting.description:
Check the ClusterResourcePlacement status:
```bash
kubectl describe clusterresourceplacement <name>
```

troubleshooting.title: Hub controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet-system
kubectl logs -l app=kubefleet-hub -n kubefleet-system
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your cluster.
